title: assistant-research Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-research skill's source, confidence, and verification contracts.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-research skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against tier selection, source confidence, URL verification, conflicts, and gaps contracts.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: Technology comparison uses standard tier
cases.purpose: Checks that a technology comparison uses the research tool, standard tier, confidence-scored findings, conflicts, and gaps.
cases.prompt: Research whether we should use shell scripts or a small .NET CLI for skill fixture validation. Compare options for maintainability.
setup_context: The assistant-research skill instructions are active.
The user asks for a technology comparison and decision support.
No external sources have been gathered yet.
expected_behavior: Resolves tool as research and tier as standard or stronger based on the comparison request.
Logs the research tier and question before search activity.
Applies an evidence budget using differentiated angles such as direct evidence, alternatives, and counter-evidence.
Treats subagent dispatch as optional and adapter-aware rather than mandatory.
Synthesizes findings with HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW confidence based on source count and quality.
Reports CONFLICTS even when none are found.
Reports GAPS for unanswered or weakly supported questions.
pass_criteria: The response names the selected tool and tier.
The response distinguishes sources from verified URLs.
The response includes conflicts and gaps sections.
The response does not present single-source findings as HIGH confidence.
fail_signals: Gives a free-form recommendation with no confidence levels.
Treats one source as HIGH confidence without justification.
Uses duplicate search angles for standard tier.
Claims a fixed number of parallel agents is mandatory in every adapter.

cases.title: URL output verifies links
cases.purpose: Checks that URLs are verified before presentation and unverified or hallucinated links are removed or flagged.
cases.prompt: Find official docs for the latest OpenAI Responses API and give me links I can rely on.
setup_context: The assistant-research skill instructions are active.
The user explicitly asks for reliable URLs.
Some candidate URLs may come from memory or model prior rather than search results.
expected_behavior: Treats reliable URLs as requiring verification before presentation.
Ensures every URL presented came from a search result or verified source.
Populates verified_urls only with links that were checked as accessible.
Removes or flags unverified URLs instead of presenting them as confirmed.
Assigns LOW confidence to findings with weak or conflicting source support.
Reports gaps if a URL cannot be verified.
pass_criteria: The response includes verified_urls or an explicit verified URLs section.
The response states that unverified URLs were removed or flagged.
The response includes source attribution for each finding.
The response includes gaps for links that could not be verified.
The response does not invent plausible-looking URLs.
fail_signals: Includes hallucinated URLs from prior knowledge.
Omits gaps when URLs could not be checked.
Claims all links are official without source evidence.

cases.title: Five-lens decision briefing uses perspective scan and peer review
cases.purpose: Checks that decision-grade research uses the five-lens method, contradiction map, synthesis, verification, and peer review instead of a one-shot summary.
cases.prompt: Research whether I should invest time in learning a new AI coding assistant framework for my .NET architecture work. I want a decision-grade briefing, not a generic summary.
setup_context: The assistant-research skill instructions are active.
The user asks for decision-grade research with practical implications.
External source access may be available but claims still need confidence and URL verification before final presentation.
expected_behavior: Resolves research_method as five_lens_briefing and tier as extensive or deep when decision risk warrants it.
Runs the five required lenses: practitioner, academic/technical expert, skeptic, economist/incentives analyst, and historian/pattern matcher.
Builds a contradiction map before synthesis.
Produces a recommendation no stronger than the verified evidence supports.
Runs peer review/self-critique and identifies weakest claim, bias, missing perspective, and falsification test.
Separates lens inference from source-backed findings and verifies decision-critical URLs before presenting them.
pass_criteria: The response names method as five_lens_briefing.
The response includes PERSPECTIVE SCAN, CONTRADICTION MAP, SYNTHESIS, PEER REVIEW, SOURCES/VERIFIED URLS, and GAPS sections.
The recommendation is do, wait, avoid, or investigate_further with confidence caveats.
Unsourced or weakly sourced lens claims are LOW confidence or listed as gaps.
fail_signals: Answers from one generic perspective only.
Presents lens speculation as verified fact.
Makes a strong recommendation without source verification.
